bianco_interrogation: Alright, alright! I'll talk! Just... keep that dinosaur away from me.
We were hired to find a girl. We thought it could be that kid, so we took her to our boss.
...you don't want to mess with him.
That's all I know, I swear!
bianco: Hey, have you heard? There's some kid wandering around the ruins again.
Apparently he plays soldier out in the desert, pretending to be some kind of warrior like his old man.
Problem is, he keeps stumbling into our operations. Blu tried to chase him off with his Guysack, but the brat won't take a hint.
Let's head to the ruins and help Bulu get rid of that nuisance once and for all.
becker_scan: Oh, a customer! People rarely buy Core Probes from me, so let me give you a tip to get the most out of them.
When you're fighting a Zoid Sleeper, click the probe before the battle ends. If you weaken it enough, you'll be able to scan its Core.
Planning to fight several in a row? Double-click and the probe will activate automatically in every battle.
Just keep in mind, probes are fragile. They break with each attempt, hit or miss. So you'll need one per scan.
But hey, no complaints from me... the more you buy, the better for business!
boy: Please, you have to help me! A bandit came and took my sister and my mother!
They went toward the abandoned camp to the north... I'm so scared for them.
But be careful, the bandit is really strong!
You should take the intermediate route to get there. You'll encounter Zoids Sleepers along the way — it's good practice for your combat techniques.
maria_fiona: You were worried about Van? He left early with that girl and that dinosaur they found. I think he's showing her around the town.
That boy... he's always getting himself into these situations.
But a girl with no memories? That's... concerning.
If you see him, please tell him to be careful. And... thank you for your help.
maria_flyheight: My brother Van? *sighs* He's out there again, isn't he?
He's always dreamed of being a great warrior like our father. He spends his days exploring the Elmia Desert, pretending he's on some grand adventure.
He's probably out there right now, wandering around the ruins.
If you're heading that way, please watch out for him. He's reckless, but he means well.
The desert is east of here. Be careful, the Sleepers out there are stronger than the ones near the village.
narration_discover_fione: After the miracle you just witnessed, you return to the ruins searching for any sign of Van.
Deep within the crumbling passages, you hear he's in a chamber similar to where they found Zeke.
Suddenly, another capsule opens and a girl emerges from it.
But at that very moment, weakened by the battle, the ground beneath you gives way and you fall into a hidden chamber.
In the center stands a small ancient statue, its surface covered in strange symbols.
narration_shield_liger: In the middle of the battle, Van bursts out of the ruins, carried by Zeke.
Bianco and Nero turn around and chase him. Your Zoids are still regrouping and you can't reach Van in time. He's cornered.
But then something strange happens: Zeke fuses with one of the destroyed Zoids from the ruins, and suddenly it rises, fully restored.
And in a matter of seconds, Van and the Shield Liger drive the bandits away.
jenkins_to_work: Well, well! So you actually managed to get a Zoid's structural data. Not bad for a rookie.
Alright, time to get to work. Let's head to my humble lab and see what we can create with what you've got.
jenkins: So Malinoff sent you? Well, well... I saw how you helped the people of this village, so I'll hear you out.
More Zoids, you say? The easiest way is buying them from the army, though they'll only sell you their scraps.
I stopped working with Zoids a long time ago, but back in my day we used traps to catch wild Zoids. Nowadays it's nearly impossible to find them, let alone get one to cooperate with you.
So the armies came up with a new method: we learned to incubate Zoid Cores.
As you know, the Zoid Core is the vital nucleus of every Zoid. Each species has a different Core, but they all share the same origin: the metals dissolved in the planet's primordial waters.

What we managed to do was recreate the conditions of those ancient waters in a laboratory. If you gather enough Zoid Magnite, or failing that, plenty of Magnis, we can submerge it in that substance and create a brand new Zoid Core.
The next step is shaping that Core into the species you need. For that, you'll need the Zoid's structural data.
Here, take this. It's a Core Analyzer. Connect it to an active Zoid Core and you can scan its structure to get the data we need to shape a new Core after that species.
There are a lot of Zoid Sleepers roaming this region lately. If you come across one, try not to wreck it and analyze its Core instead.
Fair warning though — no Zoid will just let you access its Core. You'll have to immobilize it first. I won't lie to you, taking down a Zoid without destroying it is no easy feat.
Once you've got the data from a Zoid, come see me and we'll start incubating some Cores. With this basic lab, we can only hatch simple Zoids, and I can't promise a high success rate.
One last thing: to connect the Analyzer to the Cores you'll need a Probe. They're single-use, so you'll have to stock up. Visit Mrs. Becker's workshop, she should have them.
priest_leon: Hmm, a missing girl, you say? I'm afraid we haven't heard anything about that here in Wind Colony.
But... if anyone would know about the bandits' movements, it's that boy who's always getting himself into trouble with them.
I don't know what drives him, but he's constantly venturing into the desert to confront them. Quite reckless if you ask me.
You should speak with his sister. She might know where to find him.
captain_farewell: Well, well! Now you actually look like a proper army commander.
I can see you're determined to take on those bandits. Good luck, kid.
Be careful out there. In these times of war, it's best not to get mixed up in military affairs. But something tells me it's too late for that...
captain_debrief: Thank you for your help, I knew there was something special about you...
Hmmm an artifact used by the bandits? Let me take a look...
Ohh back in my days in the army I saw these kinds of artifacts, but this one looks far more advanced than any I knew...
If I'm not mistaken, it's a Sleeper module. Some people in the army only care about the number of Zoids available for battle.
So they invented the Sleeper system as a way for pilots to control multiple Zoids at the same time.
It seems these inventions have fallen into the wrong hands, that's why there are so many Zoids Sleepers roaming around here...
I'm almost certain the bandits brought them.
But make no mistake, young pilot, not all army inventions are bad...
If you earn its trust, this Sleeper system would allow you to bond with several Zoids at the same time.
If you really plan to face those bandits, you could use some reinforcements.
For that you'll need more Zoids. Why don't you talk to old Jenkins? He used to work in a Zoid laboratory, maybe he can help you.
captain: Bandits have been raiding our village. They grow bolder each day.
All our soldiers were recruited for the war between the Republic and the Empire. There is no one left to defend us.
I'm too old to fight anymore. My body can't take the strain of piloting a Zoid.
But I still have three Zoids from my days as a soldier. They're outdated... but they always saved my life.
Would you pilot one of them to protect this village?
challenge: But choosing a Zoid isn't enough. The Zoid must choose you too.
Prove your worth. Climb up to the cockpit and show it you have the spirit of a pilot!
farewell: Ha! I knew you had it in you. The Zoid has accepted you as its pilot.
Now go and help our neighbors. The bandits won't wait forever.
Be careful out there, kid. And take good care of that Zoid.
scrap_dealer: Galos, Zi-Dollars, Zenys... all those currencies, one day they're worth a fortune, and the next they won't buy you a grain of salt.
You want some advice, kid? Keep your wealth in materials.
Me? I collect Magnis, tiny shards of Zoid Magnite you can salvage from destroyed Zoids.
They're usually concentrated around the pipes connected to the Zoid Core.
Next time you take one down, don't forget to check the wreckage. You'd be surprised what you can find.
planet_intro: On Planet Zi, beyond the Milky Way, there exist steel Zoids with outstanding combat capability. The Zoids carry within them a warrior spirit.

They are the most powerful weapon in the war between the Empire and the Republic of Planet Zi. For decades, this war has ravaged these lands. Amidst this great conflict, smaller villages struggle to survive.
In one of these forgotten villages, a new threat approaches...
van_alert: Huh? Who are you? Wait... you're not one of those bandits, are you?
My name's Van. Van Flyheight. I was hiding in here when one of those capsules opened and that Zoid came out and attacked me.
Bandits? More of them coming? I figured as much...
So you can command Zoids? Great, then let's go after them and...
No time!! Maybe I can trick that Guysack guy and escape. Don't worry about me.
woman: Thank you for saving me! But please, you have to help me!
The bandits... they took my daughter! They dragged her towards Wind Colony!
I'm desperate... she's just a child. Please, you have to save her!
Wind Colony is to the north. Be careful, the road is dangerous.
Take this, it might be useful to you.
Those bandits were using these devices to command the Zoids around this camp and keep soldiers from getting close.
I managed to snatch one from the bandit who took my daughter. I don't really know what it is, but maybe that old man in the village would know.
